title: Bridge Essay: DLPFC-PPC-cTBS effects on metacognitive awareness.
concept: Metacognition — self-monitoring, metacognitive awareness, prefrontal-parietal insight circuits.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dashboard Diagnosis** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Metacognition — self-monitoring, metacognitive awareness, prefrontal-parietal insight circuits.

**DLPFC-PPC-cTBS effects on metacognitive awareness.** - Cortex (2023). DOI: 10.1016/j.cortex.2023.05.022. PMID: 37523964. BACKGROUND: Neuroimaging and lesion studies suggested that the dorsolateral prefrontal and posterior parietal cortices mediate visual metacognitive awareness. The causal evidence provided by non-invasive brain stimulation, however, is inconsistent. OBJECTIVE/HYPOTHESIS: Here we revisit a major figure discrimination experiment adding a new Kanizsa figure task trying to resolve whether bilateral continuous theta-burst transcranial magnetic stimulation (cTBS) over these regions affects perceptual m
